summary: Backend-only testing of PDF export per-drug colors and decimal minute support. All 23 tests PASSED. Verified: (1) DRUG_COLORS array properly defined with 4 different purple shades, (2) Drug Perfusion section uses per-drug colors (#F3E8FF, #EDE9FE, etc.), (3) Drug Readout section has ONE header with multiple drug boxes in per-drug colors, (4) BF Evolution legend shows each drug individually as '[drug name] perfusion', (5) BF Evolution chart drug regions use per-drug solid colors, (6) BF table highlights use per-drug tint colors, (7) HRV table highlights use per-drug tint colors, (8) Backend supports decimal minute inputs (0.5, 1.5, 2.5, etc.) for BF and HRV readouts.
critical_code_review_comments: export_utils.py lines 31-36: DRUG_COLORS array correctly defined with 4 entries: #F3E8FF (purple), #EDE9FE (violet), #E9D5FF (fuchsia), #DDD6FE (indigo)
export_utils.py lines 265-296: Drug Perfusion section uses DRUG_COLORS[idx % len(DRUG_COLORS)]['tint'] for per-drug coloring
export_utils.py lines 343-467: Drug Readout section has single header 'DRUG READOUT' with multiple drug boxes using per-drug colors
export_utils.py lines 661-667: BF Evolution chart drug regions use DRUG_COLORS[idx]['solid'] for per-drug region overlays
export_utils.py lines 680-684: BF Evolution legend shows each drug as '[drug_name] perfusion' with per-drug colors
export_utils.py lines 1046-1054: BF table (Table 1) uses drug_window_colors dict mapping window_str to drug_idx for per-drug highlighting
export_utils.py lines 1184-1192: HRV table (Table 2) uses drug_minute_colors dict mapping minute to drug_idx for per-drug highlighting
export_utils.py lines 406-416: Decimal minute inputs supported via float() conversion then int() floor for data lookup
seed_data_creation: Created mock test data with 2 drugs (Tetrodotoxin + Acetylcholine) with decimal minute inputs (1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5), also created 4-drug and 5-drug test scenarios to verify color wraparound
context_for_next_testing_agent: PDF export per-drug colors feature fully tested and working. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_pdf_per_drug_colors.py covers: DRUG_COLORS definition unit tests, decimal minute support, 2/4/5 drug scenarios, color wraparound. Key color assignments: Drug 1 = #F3E8FF (tint) / #a855f7 (solid), Drug 2 = #EDE9FE (tint) / #8b5cf6 (solid), Drug 3 = #E9D5FF (tint) / #c084fc (solid), Drug 4 = #DDD6FE (tint) / #a78bfa (solid).

features_verified.drug_colors_definition: PASS - DRUG_COLORS array has 4 entries with tint, solid, name keys
features_verified.drug_perfusion_per_drug_colors: PASS - Each drug in Drug Perfusion section uses different tint color
features_verified.drug_readout_single_header: PASS - Single 'DRUG READOUT' header with multiple drug boxes
features_verified.drug_readout_per_drug_colors: PASS - Each drug box uses its index-based color from DRUG_COLORS
features_verified.bf_evolution_per_drug_regions: PASS - Chart drug regions use per-drug solid colors with 0.15 alpha
features_verified.bf_evolution_per_drug_legend: PASS - Legend shows '[drug_name] perfusion' for each drug
features_verified.bf_table_per_drug_highlighting: PASS - Drug readout rows use per-drug tint colors
features_verified.hrv_table_per_drug_highlighting: PASS - Drug readout rows use per-drug tint colors
features_verified.decimal_minute_support: PASS - Backend accepts 0.5, 1.5, 2.5 etc. and floors for lookup
features_verified.color_wraparound: PASS - More than 4 drugs wraps back to first color
